description: city in and county seat of Natrona County, Wyoming, United States
description_wiki: Casper is a city in, and the county seat of, Natrona County, Wyoming, United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: town in Oklahoma, USA
description_wiki: Drummond is a town in Garfield County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Garfield County, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Enid ( EE-nid) is the ninth-largest city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: military decoration of France
description_wiki: The Croix de Guerre (French: [kʁwa də ɡɛʁ], Cross of War) is a military decoration of France.

description_wiki: Bausch & Lomb (since 2010 stylized as Bausch + Lomb) is an  American Canadian eye health products company based in Vaughan, Ontario, Canada.

description: former Oral Roberts University law school
description_wiki: The O. W. Coburn School of Law was the law school of Oral Roberts University.

description: Irish composer
description_wiki: Charles Coffey (late 17th century – 13 May 1745) was an Irish playwright, opera librettist and arranger of music from County Westmeath.

description: American media mogul and minister (1930–2023)
description_wiki: Marion Gordon "Pat" Robertson (March 22, 1930 – June 8, 2023) was an American media mogul, televangelist, political commentator, presidential candidate, and charismatic minister.

description: American law professor; witness in Clarence Thomas controversy
description_wiki: Anita Faye Hill (born July 30, 1956) is an American lawyer, educator and author.

description: Associate Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States since 1991
description_wiki: Clarence Thomas (born June 23, 1948) is an American lawyer and jurist who has served since 1991 as an associate justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.

description: city in Muskogee County, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Muskogee () is the 13th-largest city in Oklahoma and is the county seat of Muskogee County.

description: public university in Oklahoma, United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma State University (informally Oklahoma State or OSU) is a public land-grant research university in Stillwater, Oklahoma, United States.

description: beauty pageant
description_wiki: The Miss Oklahoma competition selects a winner to compete on behalf of Oklahoma in the Miss America pageant.

description: Miss Oklahoma 1967
annual singing competition
description_wiki: The Metropolitan Opera Eric and Dominique Laffont Competition (formerly the Metropolitan Opera National Council Auditions) is an annual singing competition sponsored by the Metropolitan Opera.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: 1975 capture of Saigon by the People’s Army of Vietnam and the National Liberation Front of South Vietnam
description_wiki: Saigon, the capital of South Vietnam, was captured by North Vietnam on 30 April 1975.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: country in East Asia
description_wiki: South Korea, officially the Republic of Korea (ROK), is a country in East Asia.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: president of the United States from 1963 to 1969 (1908–1973)
description_wiki: Lyndon Baines Johnson (August 27, 1908 – January 22, 1973), also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States from 1963 until 1969.

description: president of the United States from 1969 to 1974 (1913–1994)
description_wiki: Richard Milhous Nixon (January 9, 1913 – April 22, 1994) was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974.

description: American businessman and Secretary of Defense (1916-2009)
description_wiki: Robert Strange McNamara (; June 9, 1916 – July 6, 2009) was an American businessman and government official who served as the eighth United States secretary of defense from 1961 to 1968 under presidents John F. Kennedy and Lyndon B. Johnson at the height of the Cold War.

description: selection of the Democratic Party nominee for President of the United States in 1968
description_wiki: The 1968 Democratic National Convention was held August 26–29 at the International Amphitheatre in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description_wiki: Agent Orange is a chemical herbicide and defoliant, one of the tactical uses of Rainbow Herbicides.

description: United States federal executive department
description_wiki: The United States Department of Justice (DOJ) is an executive department of the United States federal government that oversees the domestic enforcement of federal laws and the administration of justice.

description: country in southern Europe
description_wiki: Italy, officially the Italian Republic, is a country in Southern and Western Europe.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.
